question: Will the clinic's monthly referrals recover to their December level by mid-October 2025?

clinic.description: The medical clinic whose referral volume is being tracked.

referring_gp.description: A general practitioner who can refer patients to the clinic.

practice_manager.description: The clinic's practice manager, responsible for operational data.

decision_holder.description: A person with sole authority over fees and programs (e.g., Sam Whitfield).

monthly_referral_count.description: Recorded count of referrals for a given month, optionally broken down by source GP.

referral_source_stats_report.description: A report pulled by Kim from the practice management system showing monthly referral counts.

gp_willingness_status.description: Result of asking a GP whether they are willing to resume referrals. Values: willing, conditional, permanently_moved.

decision_record.description: A recorded decision by a decision holder (e.g., Sam Whitfield). Decision types: fee_change_reversal, referral_partner_program_launch.

fee_change_reversal_cost.description: Cost of reversing the January fee change for a given quarter. Fixed at 8000 per quarter.

referral_partner_program_cost.description: Cost of launching a referral-partner program. Fixed at 5000.

top_referrer.description: Indicates a GP was the top referrer (25/month) and stopped in February 2025.

referral_relationship_decay.description: Tracks that a GP who stays away past October is unlikely to return this year. Decay threshold is October 2025.

referral_recorded.description: A referral is logged in the practice management system.

gp_willingness_asked.description: A direct conversation occurs to determine if a GP is willing to resume referrals.

decision_recorded.description: A decision holder records a decision in the system. If not recorded within two weeks of consideration, the decision lapses.

referral_source_stats_pulled.description: Kim pulls referral-source stats from the practice management system.

unmodeled_actor_action.description: schema-scoped scaffolding: an actor action whose semantics could not be compiled; exact content preserved; counted as a fallback

referral_recovery_assessment.description: Process to determine whether monthly referrals recover to December level by mid-October 2025.

fee_change_reversal_cost_constraint.description: Reversing the January fee change costs 8000 per quarter.
fee_change_reversal_cost_constraint.rule: If fee_change_reversal is recorded, then cost = 8000 * number_of_quarters_affected.
fee_change_reversal_cost_constraint.evidence: Reversing the January fee change costs 8k/quarter.

referral_partner_program_cost_constraint.rule: If referral_partner_program_launch is recorded, then cost = 5000.

referral_relationship_decay_constraint.description: A GP who stays away past October is unlikely to return this year.
referral_relationship_decay_constraint.evidence: Referral relationships decay slowly: a GP who stays away past October is unlikely to return this year.

practice_management_system.description: Logs every referral with source and month. Monthly counts are directly measurable.

referral_source_stats_report.description: Report generated by Kim from the practice management system within one week.

monthly_referral_count_measurability.rule: Monthly referral counts are directly measurable from the practice management system against the seasonally-adjusted December baseline of 60/month.
monthly_referral_count_measurability.evidence: The practice management system logs every referral with source and month, so monthly counts are directly measurable against the seasonally-adjusted December baseline of 60/month.

gp_willingness_discoverability.rule: Whether GP Alvarez is willing to resume is unknown until someone asks him; his answer (willing / conditional / permanently moved) is discoverable by a direct conversation.
gp_willingness_discoverability.evidence: Whether he is WILLING to resume is unknown until someone asks him, and his answer (willing / conditional / permanently moved) is discoverable by a direct conversation.

sam_whitfield.why_consequential: Holds sole authority over fees and programs; any decision to reverse fee change or launch referral program requires his recorded decision.

referring_gp_alvarez.why_consequential: Was the top referrer (25/month) and stopped in February; his willingness to resume directly impacts whether referrals can recover to December level.

practice_manager_kim.why_consequential: Can pull referral-source stats within a week, enabling measurement of current referral levels.

outcome_predicates.option_true: Yes, referrals recover to December level (60/month) by mid-October 2025.
outcome_predicates.option_false: No, referrals do not recover to December level by mid-October 2025.

unresolved_mechanisms: Whether GP Alvarez will be asked about willingness before the horizon (2025-10-16).
Whether Sam Whitfield will make any decision within the two-week lapse window.
Whether any conditional response from GP Alvarez includes conditions that Sam Whitfield can or will meet.
institution sam_whitfield_authority dropped: no decision holders declared
institution decision_lapse_rule dropped: no decision holders declared
assumptions: The seasonally-adjusted December baseline of 60/month is the correct target for recovery.
No other GPs besides Alvarez significantly affect the monthly total (i.e., Alvarez's 25/month is the key variable).
The practice management system data is accurate and up-to-date as of prediction time.
Mid-October 2025 is interpreted as October 16, 2025 (the horizon date).

missing_decisive_elements: no entity for the patient population or referral demand (e.g., seasonal illness patterns, local health trends) that could drive or suppress referrals independent of GP willingness
no entity for the clinic's capacity or appointment availability, which could cap referral acceptance even if GPs refer
no entity for competing clinics or alternative referral destinations that might divert GPs
no entity for the practice management system itself (e.g., software bugs, data lag, reporting errors) that could distort monthly_referral_count
no entity for the 'mid-October' date boundary — the model has no calendar or time progression mechanism to determine if recovery occurs by that specific date
relabeled_progress_bars: gp_willingness_asked → 'GP willingness inquiry conducted' (but no bar for whether the GP actually resumes, only willingness status)
decision_recorded → 'Decision recorded by Sam' (but no bar for whether the decision is implemented or effective before mid-October)
monthly_referral_count_updated → 'Referral count updated' (but no bar for the count reaching 60, only that an update event occurs)
hidden_answer_risks: the model assumes that if GPs are 'willing' they will immediately refer at prior rates, ignoring ramp-up time, patient backlog, or scheduling friction
the model treats 'decision recorded' as sufficient for outcome, but Sam's decision could be to do nothing, and the model has no explicit 'no decision' path
the model has no mechanism for partial recovery (e.g., 55 referrals) — the outcome predicate is a binary threshold, but the process may never produce a count that triggers the predicate
the model does not account for the possibility that the December level (60) was anomalous (e.g., one-time event) and not a sustainable baseline
direct_human_reaction_encodings: gp_willingness_asked encodes a direct conversation but does not capture that a GP might lie, change their mind later, or be influenced by peer behavior
decision_recorded encodes Sam's action but not his reasoning, pressure from stakeholders, or the possibility of a decision being reversed after recording
referral_source_stats_pulled encodes Kim pulling data but not that Kim might misinterpret or selectively report the stats
public_posture_vs_private_reality_confla: gp_willingness_status is treated as a factual state, but a GP might publicly state 'willing' while privately intending to refer only a few patients
decision_recorded is treated as authoritative, but Sam might publicly announce a reversal while privately delaying implementation
monthly_referral_count is treated as objective, but the practice management system could be gamed or contain errors that differ from ground truth
missing_nonhuman_mechanisms: no mechanism for seasonal variation in referral rates (e.g., flu season, school holidays)
no mechanism for insurance or reimbursement changes that could affect referral behavior
no mechanism for electronic health record system outages or data transfer delays that could prevent referrals from being recorded
no mechanism for regulatory or compliance changes that could block or mandate referrals
